title: Distribution message encoding — ETF wire format
purpose: Distribution messages are encoded using the External Term Format (ETF) — the same binary format used by term_to_binary/binary_to_term. Every term type (atoms, integers, tuples, lists, maps, PIDs, references, binaries) has a defined wire encoding. This brief implements the encoder and decoder for the full term type catalog.
task: Implement ETF encoding (Term → bytes) and decoding (bytes → Term) for all term types used in distribution.

requirements.title: ETF encoder — Term to bytes
requirements.spec: Encode a Term to its ETF binary representation.
acceptance: Encodes: SMALL_INTEGER_EXT, INTEGER_EXT, ATOM_UTF8_EXT, SMALL_TUPLE_EXT, LARGE_TUPLE_EXT, NIL_EXT, LIST_EXT, BINARY_EXT, NEW_FLOAT_EXT, MAP_EXT, NEW_PID_EXT, NEWER_REFERENCE_EXT
Test: encode each term type, verify byte output matches BEAM's encoding

requirements.title: ETF decoder — bytes to Term
requirements.spec: Decode an ETF binary representation into a Term, allocating boxed terms on a provided heap.
acceptance: decode_term(bytes: &[u8], heap: &mut Heap, atom_table: &AtomTable) -> Result<Term, DecodeError>
Heap allocation for boxed terms (tuples, lists, maps, binaries)
Invalid input returns DecodeError, not panic
Test: round-trip encode/decode for each term type

requirements.title: Distribution message framing
requirements.spec: Wrap encoded terms in the distribution message frame: 4-byte length prefix + pass-through header + control message + optional payload.
acceptance: write_dist_message(control: &[u8], payload: Option<&[u8]>) -> Vec<u8> produces a framed message
read_dist_message(stream) -> Result<(Vec<u8>, Option<Vec<u8>>), Error> reads and deframes
Test: frame a message, deframe it, verify content

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement COMPRESSED_EXT (zlib compression) — optimization for later
SHALL NOT implement ATOM_CACHE_REF encoding here — that's B-116's responsibility
SHALL implement the NEW_PID_EXT and NEWER_REFERENCE_EXT formats (not legacy V4_PORT_EXT)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
